purpose: Make Kernel Sentinel enforce the repo-wide usability, reliability, and simplicity doctrine as live system-health pressure instead of treating it as advisory prose.

laws.question: Does this change, subsystem, finding, or TODO help a user or agent complete concrete work?
Does the useful path keep working and fail with bounded actionable diagnostics?
Does this make the system easier to reason about, or does it add entropy?

promotion_rules: A doctrine violation may be promoted only when backed by live evidence, recurring issue evidence, or explicit operator observation.
A doctrine violation must identify the smallest real-work path it damages.
Simplicity findings must name the duplicate representation, ownership ambiguity, or compatibility tail they reduce.
Usability findings must identify the missing user-facing or agent-facing workflow.
Reliability findings must include a bounded validation or recovery probe.
